get.description: Retrieve data for a specific container

blobCreate.description: The name of the new or existing blob

binaryPropertyName.description: The name of the input binary field containing the file to be written

url.description: URL where to read of the blob contents from

accessTier.description: The tier to be set on the blob. For detailed information about block blob tiering, see Hot, cool, and archive storage tiers.

blobType.description: Specifies the type of blob to create: block, page, or append blob

cacheControl.description: Sets the blob's cache control value

contentCrc64.description: CRC64 hash of the blob content to verify its integrity during transport

contentMd5.description: Sets the blob's MD5 hash for integrity verification

encryptionContext.description: If the value is set it will set blob system metadata. Max length-1024. Valid only when Hierarchical Namespace is enabled for the account.

encryptionScope.description: Indicates the encryption scope for encrypting the request contents

expiryOption.description: Specifies the expiration date option for the request. This header is valid for accounts with hierarchical namespace enabled.

NeverExpire.description: Sets the blob to never expire or removes the current expiration date

expiryTime.description: Specifies the time when the blob is set to expire as an absolute time

immutabilityPolicyUntilDate.description: Specifies the retention-until date to be set on the blob. This is the date until which the blob can be protected from being modified or deleted.

immutabilityPolicyMode.description: Specifies the immutability policy mode to be set on the blob

leaseId.description:
If this header is specified, the operation is performed only if both of the following conditions are met:
  • The blob's lease is currently active.
  • The lease ID that's specified in the request matches the lease ID of the blob.

legalHold.description: Whether to set a legal hold on the blob

metadata.description: A name-value pair to associate with the container as metadata

fieldName.description: Names must adhere to the naming rules for C# identifiers
Names must adhere to the naming rules for C# identifiers

origin.description: Specifies the origin from which the request is issued. The presence of this header results in cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) headers on the response.

tags.description: Sets the given tags on the blob

simplify.description: Whether to return a simplified version of the response instead of the raw data

upn.description: Whether the user identity values that are returned in the response will be transformed from Microsoft Entra object IDs to User Principal Names. If the value is false, they're returned as Microsoft Entra object IDs. Valid for accounts with hierarchical namespace enabled.

fields.description: The fields to add to the output

filter.description: Filters the results to return only containers with a name that begins with the specified prefix

blob.description: Specifies public read access for blobs. Blob data within this container can be read via anonymous request, but container data isn't available. Clients can't enumerate blobs within the container via anonymous request.

container.description: Specifies full public read access for container and blob data. Clients can enumerate blobs within the container via anonymous request, but they can't enumerate containers within the storage account.

options.description: Container data is private to the account owner

returnAll.description: Whether to return all results or only up to a given limit

limit.description: Max number of results to return

containerCreate.description: The name of the new container

batchSize.description: Input will be split in batches to throttle requests. -1 for disabled. 0 will be treated as 1.

batchInterval.description: Time (in milliseconds) between each batch of requests. 0 for disabled.

batchSize.description: Input will be split in batches to throttle requests. -1 for disabled. 0 will be treated as 1.

batchInterval.description: Time (in milliseconds) between each batch of requests. 0 for disabled.

allowUnauthorizedCerts.description: Whether to accept the response even if SSL certificate validation is not possible

proxy.description: HTTP proxy to use. If authentication is required it can be defined as follow: http://username:password@myproxy:3128

timeout.description: Time in ms to wait for the server to send response headers (and start the response body) before aborting the request
